strategicView.summary: S4 SOO redteam workflow is approved with a tracked deviation: direct thin SOO jobs use an explicit framework SHA pin so environment-scoped redteam secrets can be enforced.

questions: Is the reusable-workflow preference explicitly superseded by the stronger environment-scoped secret requirement?
Will one agent path change prove only the matching redteam job runs in GitHub Actions?
Do direct thin SOO jobs keep framework/package execution pinned to an explicit SHA?
Can S5 prove the redteam environment contains staging-only LiveKit, Langfuse, OpenAI, DB, and Slack secrets without printing sensitive values?
Can S5 confirm support-agent-maya emits Langfuse correlation metadata despite using MAYA_LANGFUSE_* internally?
Does write-results plus enforce-threshold stay blocked from trusted live acceptance until S6/S7 DB readiness is proven?
Are no prod/canary triggers present before explicit later phase approval?
Do fork PRs skip secret-backed jobs as intended?
Do operational metrics remain pending until non-stub workflow evidence exists?
Can release readiness be blocked unless PostHog flagging, non-stub E2E evidence, cost guardrail proof, and security reviews are complete?
Are cost, latency, scalability, reliability, API-outage behavior, and bottlenecks measured before each phase is closed?
Will Strategic View triage reviewer findings and vt-agent-redteam cutover into fix-now work versus post-v0 backlog before any final report?
Is the v0.1.0 tag visible and installable for consumers?
Did the package metadata and CLI version agree on 0.1.0?
Is a signed tag required by release policy before SOO consumes this?
Was a real green fixture workflow recorded before closing F6?
Does the reusable workflow preserve manifest validation and stable REDTEAM_RUN_ID?
Which downstream guardrails remain for live secrets, write-results, and schema rollout?
Do PR/deploy/canary modes default to the Langfuse native transcript path?
Does dry-run remain local and safe for fixture validation?
Does non-dry-run fail fast when Langfuse credentials are missing?
Are prompt and response fields redacted before persistence?
Is response_hash computed before redaction for audit stability?
Can expected-behavior allowlists preserve intentionally required spans?
Does P0 remain non-bypassable even with an override?
Are overrides read independently from result writes?
Are transient failures classified below true policy failures?
Does the manifest distinguish full coverage from partial-no-tool-use?
Can scenario_selection exclude unsupported tags without hiding coverage gaps?
Is the coverage state visible before SOO adoption?
Is S5 the right next control point with 0/4 criteria complete?
Are Ask First boundaries still protected before secrets, database, or deploy work?
Does the runner use agent-native transcript evidence instead of synthetic replay?
Is ADR-001 still the decision source for this path?
Are plan constraints still intact before consumer-repo rollout?
Does any new deviation require an ADR before implementation?

operationalReadiness.serviceGuarantee: The service is kept healthy by phase-end fitness checks: deterministic tests, non-stub integration/E2E runs, cost guardrail proof, latency budgets, API-outage simulations, concurrency/load checks, and bottleneck triage. Until those checks are measured, the dashboard marks the phase as partial or pending.

summaryCards.label: Operational closure
summaryCards.detail: Phase 1A has framework tests and workflow evidence, but not the full phase-end operational package.

summaryCards.label: Cost guardrail
summaryCards.detail: Needs integration proof for max_cost_usd_per_run and budget_exhausted behavior.

summaryCards.label: API outage behavior
summaryCards.detail: Langfuse timeout is unit-tested; OpenAI Moderation outage currently degrades to inconclusive/pass.

summaryCards.label: Concurrency/load
summaryCards.detail: Scenario-level concurrency and many simultaneous checks still need measurement.

phaseGates.evidence: 104 pytest tests, green reusable workflow fixture, v0.1.0 tag install proof.

checks.name: Cost
checks.metric: estimated_cost_usd exists in run_summary.json; guardrail proof missing
checks.next: Add integration test for max_cost_usd_per_run -> budget_exhausted.

checks.name: Latency
checks.metric: Langfuse polling timeout paths unit-tested; no p50/p95 phase metric yet
checks.next: Capture scenario duration p50/p95 from non-stub run_summary.

checks.name: Scalability
checks.metric: Harness scenarios execute sequentially in one process
checks.next: Run controlled concurrency/load test for parallel checks.

checks.name: Reliability
checks.metric: 104 tests passed; fixture GitHub Action passed
checks.next: Extend to non-stub SOO integration evidence.

checks.name: API outage
checks.metric: Langfuse no-trace timeout returns stub-marked timeout; OpenAI outage passes inconclusive
checks.next: Decide whether OpenAI outage should fail closed, fail open, or degrade with explicit warning.

checks.name: Bottlenecks
checks.metric: Known candidates: Langfuse polling, LiveKit room creation, OpenAI scorer calls, Postgres writes, workflow fan-out
checks.next: Measure each candidate during Phase 1B/1C runs.

phaseGates.evidence: S1, S2, and S3 manifests validate locally. S4 adds a SOO redteam workflow with path-scoped jobs for all three agents, redteam environment binding, explicit framework SHA pin, staging-only target environment, fork-PR skip guard, and no prod/canary trigger. Non-stub runtime evidence still pending.

checks.name: Cost
checks.metric: S1/S2/S3 manifest budgets remain max_cost_usd_per_run=5.00 and max_scenarios_per_pr=12; S4 workflow uploads per-agent run summaries, but no non-stub provider cost has been measured.
checks.next: Use S5/S7 live probe to record actual run_summary cost per SOO agent and prove budget_exhausted behavior.

checks.name: Latency
checks.metric: S4 workflow has 15-minute per-agent job limits and summary artifacts. Real LiveKit/Langfuse p50/p95 is not measured.
checks.next: Record p50/p95 per scenario and total workflow time from the first non-stub SOO workflow.

checks.name: Scalability
checks.metric: Three path-scoped SOO jobs exist. PR path filters should fan out only touched agents; workflow_call runs all three.
checks.next: Prove path scoping in Actions with one single-agent path change and one workflow-file change.

checks.name: Reliability
checks.metric: All three manifests validate locally; S4 workflow YAML parses; framework package is pinned to an explicit SHA.
checks.next: Configure redteam environment secrets and capture first SOO Actions run evidence.

checks.name: API outage
checks.metric: Langfuse/OpenAI/LiveKit/Postgres/Slack outage scenarios. S4 wires the path but does not exercise outages.
checks.next: Run failure injection or mocked outage tests after S5/S7 readiness.

checks.name: Bottlenecks
checks.metric: Known bottleneck candidates now include GitHub job fan-out, framework package install, LiveKit dispatch, Langfuse trace search, OpenAI scoring, and DB writes.
checks.next: Record slowest stage from SOO run.

checks.next: Strategic View triages any unresolved cost gap.

failureModes.name: Langfuse API does not respond
failureModes.currentBehavior: The Langfuse runner backs off until timeout. If no trace appears, it returns a stub-marked timeout result. Stub presence forces the gate into the stub/incomplete path instead of silently passing real coverage.
failureModes.evidence: prototype/tests/test_langfuse_runner.py covers timeout and no-match paths.
failureModes.gap: Needs non-stub SOO outage simulation and latency metrics.

failureModes.name: OpenAI Moderation API does not respond
failureModes.currentBehavior: The scorer currently returns passed=True with error detail, treating the result as inconclusive/pass.
failureModes.gap: Strategic decision needed: fail closed, fail open with explicit warning, or degrade with separate gate state.

failureModes.name: LiveKit API does not respond
failureModes.currentBehavior: LiveKit room creation/dispatch paths are expected to surface dispatch errors and scenario timeouts, but the release dashboard has no measured outage evidence yet.
failureModes.evidence: LiveKit runner paths create/delete rooms and rely on scenario timeout budgets.
failureModes.gap: Need failure injection for room creation, dispatch timeout, and cleanup failure.

failureModes.name: Postgres/Supabase write path does not respond
failureModes.currentBehavior: Result persistence is required for durable evidence and dashboards. Current dashboard does not yet show measured write-failure behavior for consumer runs.
failureModes.evidence: Postgres/Supabase writer code exists with redaction before write.
failureModes.gap: Need integration test for write outage, permission denial, and recovery/disposition.

failureModes.name: Slack webhook does not respond
failureModes.currentBehavior: Slack alerting is a rollout-control channel. Alert failure must not hide gate failure, but evidence is not captured yet.
failureModes.evidence: Slack alert wiring is tracked in Phase 1C and final governance.
failureModes.gap: Need controlled alert failure test and expected fallback/reporting behavior.

failureModes.name: Many checks run at the same time
failureModes.currentBehavior: One harness process runs scenarios sequentially. GitHub Actions can fan out by agent/job, but scenario-level parallelism and provider limits are not measured yet.
failureModes.evidence: Harness loop processes scenarios one by one.
failureModes.gap: Need controlled concurrency test and max safe parallel jobs.

bottlenecks.name: Langfuse trace polling
bottlenecks.why: Every non-stub agent-native transcript depends on trace ingestion and metadata search latency.
bottlenecks.measurement: p50/p95 trace search time and timeout rate.

bottlenecks.name: LiveKit room dispatch
bottlenecks.why: Room creation and agent dispatch are external calls with timeout and cleanup requirements.
bottlenecks.measurement: room creation time, dispatch success rate, cleanup failure rate.

bottlenecks.name: OpenAI scorer calls
bottlenecks.why: Moderation API failures currently degrade to inconclusive/pass.
bottlenecks.measurement: API error rate, added latency, disposition by gate.

bottlenecks.name: Database writes
bottlenecks.why: Durable evidence and dashboards depend on Postgres/Supabase writes after redaction.
bottlenecks.measurement: write latency, write failure rate, permission failures.

bottlenecks.name: Workflow fan-out
bottlenecks.why: Multiple agents and PR paths can trigger concurrent checks; provider quotas and CI minutes may become the limiting factor.
bottlenecks.measurement: parallel job count, total CI time, provider 429s.

overview.title: Framework boundary
overview.detail: This repo owns the reusable red-team runner, manifest schema, corpus, scoring, storage, and release workflow.

overview.title: SOO boundary
overview.detail: student-onboarding-orchestration consumes the framework for the three LiveKit agents selected for v0.

overview.title: Production moderation
overview.detail: Existing app-level input moderation is not replaced; the MVP validates agent output behavior and deploy safety.

overview.title: Decision posture
overview.detail: Hard constraints, ADRs, Ask First rules, and bilingual-doc conventions remain binding for every new step.

risks.title: Langfuse correlation metadata
risks.detail: Plan assumes redteam_run_id and redteam_scenario_id metadata. SOO tracing may need explicit metadata wiring before runner search works reliably.

risks.title: SOO Ask First boundaries
risks.detail: DB, auth, deploy, infrastructure, and destructive operations require explicit confirmation inside the consumer repo.

risks.title: Maya tool-use coverage gap
risks.detail: support-agent must declare partial-no-tool-use until scenario/tool traces are proven.

risks.title: Supabase deploy-killer rules
risks.detail: Conversation Club schema work must follow local migration rules and avoid direct remote mutations.

risks.title: Deferred audio and BI scope
risks.detail: WAV collector D1 and BI dashboard D9 remain post-v0 unless priorities change through a new ADR.
